narrative: OMX30 faces a crosswind this morning following sharp European selloff (DAX -1.94%, CAC -1.59%, FTSE -1.82%) that correlates strongly with Swedish equities at r=0.85-0.89. US markets closed modestly positive (+0.77% S&P 500), but VIX spiked 10% and US 10Y yield fell 45bp to 4.461%, signaling a risk-off pivot. While OMX30 trades +5.8% above 200-MA in bull regime and USD/SEK strength (+1.35%) benefits heavy industrials and exporters, technical deterioration is concerning: oversold Stochastic (K=16.6), MACD below signal, and ADX moderate with bearish lean suggest exhaustion. Oil +2.41% adds input cost headwinds to Industrials and Consumer sectors. Expect opening weakness tracking Europe, with support at 3085 (lower Bollinger) and 3123 (yesterday close) as key holds.
morningBrief: OMX30 set to open lower, tracking European weakness (DAX -1.94%, CAC -1.59%) with high beta correlation. VIX +10% and falling US yields signal risk-off rotation; USD/SEK +1.35% offers some offset for exporters but insufficient to overcome negative cross-asset sentiment. Stochastic deeply oversold (K=16.6, D=22.4)—watch for intraday bounce if support holds at 3085 or 3123. Key technical: MACD below signal and ADX 21.4 (moderate bearish) reduce conviction in bounce scenario. Earnings/macro updates into US Jobs Claims at 14:30 CET could be catalyst. Range-trade expectation 3085–3140 until clear directional catalyst. Monitor Volvo B, Atlas Copco A (strong USD/SEK positive) vs. SSAB A, H&M B (oil/FX negatives).
drivers: European equity selloff (-1.59% to -1.94%) with high correlation (r=0.85-0.89) to OMX30
OMX30 oversold (Stochastic K=16.6, RSI=37.64) signals mean reversion risk despite bull regime
USD strength (+1.35% USD/SEK) supports exporters but offset by risk-off sentiment and VIX spike (+10.08%)
Oil +2.41% pressures industrials (28% of index) and consumer spending despite FX benefit to exporters
Moderate ADX (21.4) with bearish DI bias and MACD below signal reduce conviction

sectorImpacts.reason: Oil +2.41% raises input costs, offsetting 70% of USD/SEK export benefit (+1.35%). Risk-off sentiment dampens machinery/industrial demand.
US 10Y yield fell 45bp to 4.461%; lower rates compress NIM expectations despite overnight risk-off typically supporting Nordic banks defensively.
Risk-off rotation, VIX spike (+10%), and falling yields favor defensive sectors. Flight-to-quality supports large-cap pharma and consumer staples.
NASDAQ +0.88% but sentiment fragile; falling yields and VIX spike pressure DCF valuations. Tech remains vulnerable in risk-off context.
Copper -3.94% dominates; gold -2.61% also negative. Risk-off commodity selloff outweighs any USD/SEK support.
Oil +2.41% pressures consumer spending; EUR/SEK +0.56% and weak EUR/USD worsen H&M sourcing costs. Risk-off sentiment hurts discretionary.
Falling US 10Y yield (-45bp) to 4.461% slightly eases refinancing cost pressures, but risk-off sentiment and European weakness create headwind.

watchList.reason: USD/SEK +1.35% is strong tailwind for exports. If European weakness stabilizes and oversold Stochastic bounces, Volvo offers leverage to industrials recovery.
Export-sensitive, USD/SEK strength positive, but oil headwinds and risk-off sentiment create near-term drag. Watch for support around 50-MA (3180 for index).
Defensive rotation into risk-off environment. Falling yields also support large-cap pharma valuations. Relative strength play if OMX30 weakness persists.
Copper -3.94% is sharp and dominant; gold -2.61% also negative. Risk-off commodity selloff overpowers any USD benefit. Vulnerable if copper breaks support.
EUR/SEK +0.56% worsens sourcing costs in euro-denominated supply chain. Oil +2.41% pressures consumer spending. Double headwind on weak euro and energy costs.
US 10Y yield fell 45bp; NIM compression risk outweighs defensive appeal. Risk-off typically supports financials, but rate-sensitive Nordic banks vulnerable to yield drop.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (data-dependent risk). No major Swedish economic data. Monitor UK political developments (Burnham/Starmer tension per FT) for GBP/SEK spillover if volatility escalates.
topRisk: Further European equity capitulation (DAX/CAC below support) triggers stop-loss cascade in OMX30; VIX >20 and falling yields pressure tech/growth. UK political turmoil could spike volatility and FX moves. Stochastic oversold invites aggressive short-covering rally that could break 3140 resistance just as quickly.
technicalSummary: Bull regime (OMX30 +5.8% vs 200-MA) but near-term momentum exhausted (MACD below signal)
Stochastic K=16.6 (deeply oversold, mean reversion bias)
Bollinger %B=0.17 (price in lower half, support at 3085)
ROC-90 +5.2% shows strong underlying momentum, contradicting short-term weakness (divergence warning)
